entries.title: IBM outlines Kookaburra / Blue Jay path toward 100k+ physical qubits
Quantinuum H2 trapped-ion system passes 56 physical qubits at high fidelity
IBM debuts its 133-qubit Heron processor and Quantum System Two, its first modular quantum computer
Atom Computing unveils a 1,225-site neutral-atom array populated with 1,180 qubits, the first gate-based system to exceed 1,000 qubits
Harvard, QuEra, MIT and NIST/University of Maryland run error-corrected algorithms on 48 logical qubits using a 280-atom neutral-atom array
Google's 105-qubit Willow chip demonstrates error correction below the surface-code threshold, with logical error rates falling as qubit count scales up
Microsoft unveils Majorana 1, its first quantum processor built with topological qubits, with 8 qubits on a chip designed to scale to one million
PsiQuantum unveils Omega, a photonic quantum computing chipset manufactured at a GlobalFoundries commercial semiconductor fab in New York
USTC's Jiuzhang 3.0 photonic quantum computer detects 255 photons in a Gaussian boson sampling experiment, claiming an advantage over classical supercomputers
Origin Quantum launches Wukong, China's first homegrown 72-qubit superconducting quantum computer, for public cloud access
China's Hefei-based superconducting quantum computer production line expands capacity to assemble 8 devices simultaneously, up from 5
entries.org: Anhui Quantum Computing Engineering Research Center
entries.orgId: anhui quantum computing engineering research center

entries.title: China Telecom, CAS and QuantumCTek unveil Tianyan-504, a quantum computer powered by the 504-qubit Xiaohong superconducting chip
USTC's 105-qubit Zuchongzhi-3 processor claims a random circuit sampling speed roughly a quadrillion times faster than the fastest supercomputer
Oxford Quantum Circuits launches Toshiko, a 32-qubit platform deployed directly inside a commercial data centre
IQM announces its Radiance roadmap, targeting a 150-qubit quantum computer for enterprise and HPC customers
Pasqal loads more than 1,000 rubidium atoms in a single shot in its neutral-atom quantum processor
VTT and IQM launch Europe's first 50-qubit superconducting quantum computer at Micronova in Espoo, Finland
Alice & Bob reports cat-qubit bit-flip lifetimes exceeding one hour, up from its prior 430-second record on the Boson 4 chip
Fujitsu and RIKEN unveil a 256-qubit superconducting quantum computer, quadrupling qubit density within the same dilution refrigerator as their 64-qubit predecessor
Xanadu's Aurora photonic quantum computer networks 35 photonic chips across four server racks using 13 km of fiber optics at room temperature
D-Wave completes calibration and benchmarking of its 4,400-plus-qubit Advantage2 annealing quantum processor
QpiAI launches QpiAI-Indus, India's first full-stack 25-qubit superconducting quantum computer, under India's National Quantum Mission
Silicon Quantum Computing publishes an 11-qubit silicon processor in Nature with two-qubit gate fidelities up to 99.99% that improve as qubit count scales
AWS Braket adds new hardware backends for on-demand access
IonQ signs a $54.5 million contract with the Air Force Research Laboratory for quantum networking research, its largest 2024 US quantum award
DARPA selects Quantinuum to advance to Stage A of its Quantum Benchmarking Initiative
NERSC at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory opens cloud access to IBM quantum computers through a new Quantum Innovation Center
DARPA advances 11 companies, including IBM, IonQ and Quantinuum, to Stage B of its Quantum Benchmarking Initiative
Chinese Academy of Sciences researchers deliver a 504-qubit superconducting chip to QuantumCTek to build a cloud-accessible quantum computer with China Telecom
Origin Quantum's Wukong computer surpasses 20 million cumulative remote visits from users in 139 countries since its January 2024 launch
China Telecom Quantum Group and QuantumCTek put the Zuchongzhi superconducting quantum computer into commercial operation via the Tianyan cloud platform
Origin Quantum unveils its fourth-generation Wukong-180 quantum computer, after its predecessor logged about 50 million cloud accesses from over 160 countries
Pasqal delivers two 100+-qubit neutral-atom quantum computers to GENCI/CEA in France and FZJ in Germany under the EuroHPC-funded HPCQS project
IQM opens its first quantum data centre in Munich, Germany, with plans to host up to 12 quantum computers for industry customers
EuroHPC JU and IQM sign a procurement contract for the Euro-Q-Exa superconducting quantum computer to be hosted in Germany
EuroHPC JU and Qilimanjaro Quantum Tech sign a procurement contract for the MareNostrum Ona quantum annealer to be hosted in Spain
UK's National Quantum Computing Centre awards contracts to seven companies to deliver quantum computing testbeds under a £30 million programme
Australian and Queensland governments commit $940 million AUD to PsiQuantum to build a utility-scale fault-tolerant quantum computer near Brisbane
Fujitsu receives an order for a gate-based superconducting quantum computer from Japan's National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology
Yonsei University deploys the first IBM Quantum System One in South Korea, only the second such system on a university campus worldwide
IBM and RIKEN unveil the first IBM Quantum System Two deployed outside the United States, co-located with the Fugaku supercomputer in Kobe, Japan
Xanadu is selected for Canada's Quantum Champions Program and will receive up to CAD $23 million in federal funding
Automated calibration cuts bring-up time for a 21-qubit QuantWare Contralto processor at Israel's IQCC to about 15 minutes
QuantWare raises a $178 million Series B to build KiloFab, a Delft quantum-chip factory, and scale its VIO-40K architecture toward 10,000-qubit processors
QuTech publishes QARPET, a crossbar test-chip architecture that characterizes hundreds of semiconductor spin qubits under real operating conditions, in Nature Electronics
SDT opens South Korea's first commercial quantum-AI hybrid data center in Seoul, running its 20-qubit superconducting Kreo processor integrated with NVIDIA DGX B200 via NVQLink
Academia Sinica announces it has built a 20-qubit superconducting quantum computer, Taiwan's most advanced to date

ETH Zurich and the Paul Scherrer Institute demonstrate fault-tolerant lattice surgery on superconducting qubits, correcting errors during an operation between logical qubits, published in Nature Physics

ETH Zurich demonstrates a quantum computer architecture that stores information in mechanical resonators coupled to superconducting qubits and runs a quantum Fourier transform on it, published in Science
Horizon Quantum buys a 256-qubit trapped-ion system from IonQ, adding a second hardware modality to its Singapore testbed alongside its existing superconducting processor
Alpine Quantum Technologies unveils its LYNX Series trapped-ion quantum computer, reaching a record Quantum Volume of 32,768, designed and built in Innsbruck
eleQtron closes a €57 million Series A, backed by a €54 million order book, to expand production of its MAGIC-technology trapped-ion quantum computers
EuroHPC JU inaugurates EuroQCS-Spain, a 10-qubit analogue quantum annealer at the Barcelona Supercomputing Center integrated with the MareNostrum 5 supercomputer

entries.title: Aramco and Pasqal launch Saudi Arabia's first quantum computer, a 200-qubit neutral-atom system, and the Middle East's first commercial Quantum Computing as a Service platform, at Aramco's Dhahran data center
IQM sells a 54-qubit Radiance quantum computer to Polish IT firm Galaxy Systemy Informatyczne, the first private company anywhere to buy and operate its own on-premise quantum system

entries.title: Taiwan's National Science and Technology Council opens an international tender to procure a quantum computer for its national HPC-quantum mainframe project, with US export restrictions a live constraint on bidders
entries.orgId: national science and technology council taiwan

entries.title: Quantinuum opens an R&D and Operations Centre in Singapore under its National Quantum Office partnership, ahead of a planned deployment of a Helios quantum computer for local researchers and companies

Pasqal delivers a 140-qubit neutral-atom quantum processor to Italy's CINECA supercomputing center in Bologna, to be integrated with the Leonardo pre-exascale supercomputer under the national ICSC program
CINECA inaugurates SOL, the 140-qubit Pasqal Orion neutral-atom quantum processor delivered in February, as Italy's first operational neutral-atom quantum computer
EuroHPC JU inaugurates Euro-Q-Exa, a 54-qubit IQM superconducting quantum computer integrated with the SuperMUC-NG supercomputer at Germany's Leibniz Supercomputing Centre in Munich
entries.org: EuroHPC JU / IQM / Leibniz Supercomputing Centre

entries.title: Abu Dhabi's Technology Innovation Institute launches a cloud service giving external partners remote access to its in-house 5-to-25-qubit superconducting quantum processors via the open-source Qibo framework
KISTI signs a trilateral agreement with NVIDIA and IonQ to build a national quantum-HPC hybrid environment integrating a 100-qubit IonQ Tempo system with Korea's HANGANG supercomputer

IBM confirms Amaravati's Quantum Valley Tech Park will host a 156-qubit IBM Quantum System Two, one of the first two IBM quantum computers physically deployed in India, targeted for commissioning by September 2026

entries.orgId: ibm government of andhra pradesh tcs

entries.title: Brazil's federal science ministry and the Paraíba state government confirm CIQuanta, a João Pessoa center that will house Brazil's first two operational quantum computers, 20- and 100-qubit systems

entries.title: D-Wave launches the 2000Q, a 2,000-qubit quantum annealer, with cybersecurity firm Temporal Defense Systems as its first system order
IBM announces a working 50-qubit quantum processor prototype and makes a 20-qubit system commercially available via the cloud.
Google unveils Bristlecone, a 72-qubit superconducting quantum processor, at the APS March Meeting, extending its 9-qubit predecessor architecture.
IBM unveils IBM Q System One at CES, the world's first integrated quantum computing system designed for commercial and scientific use outside the research lab.

UNSW and Silicon Quantum Computing demonstrate the world's fastest two-qubit gate in silicon, completing the operation in 0.8 nanoseconds, about 200 times faster than prior silicon spin-qubit gates, with 98% fidelity.
Google's 53-qubit Sycamore processor completes a random-circuit-sampling task in about 200 seconds, the first published quantum supremacy claim.
Origin Quantum puts its first-generation Wuyuan 6-qubit superconducting quantum computer on the Origin Quantum Cloud, China's first quantum computing cloud platform.
D-Wave launches Advantage, its fifth-generation annealing quantum computer with more than 5,000 qubits and 15-way qubit connectivity
Honeywell launches System Model H1, its first commercial trapped-ion computer with 10 fully-connected qubits and a Quantum Volume of 128
USTC's Jiuzhang photonic quantum computer detects 76 photons via Gaussian boson sampling, achieving the first photonic quantum computational advantage.
entries.org: University of Science and Technology of China (USTC)
entries.orgId: university of science and technology of china ustc

entries.title: USTC unveils Zuchongzhi, a 62-qubit programmable superconducting quantum processor, then the largest qubit count of any device in the world.
entries.org: University of Science and Technology of China (USTC)
entries.orgId: university of science and technology of china ustc

entries.title: Honeywell's System Model H1-1 reaches a Quantum Volume of 1,024, a 16-fold increase in one year
USTC's 66-qubit Zuchongzhi 2.1 processor achieves superconducting quantum computational advantage, sampling random circuits about 10 million times faster than the fastest supercomputer.
entries.org: University of Science and Technology of China (USTC), Chinese Academy of Sciences
entries.orgId: university of science and technology of china ustc chinese academy of sciences

entries.title: USTC's upgraded Jiuzhang 2.0 photonic processor detects 113 photons, pushing its computational speedup to septillions of times faster than classical supercomputers.
entries.org: University of Science and Technology of China (USTC), Chinese Academy of Sciences
entries.orgId: university of science and technology of china ustc chinese academy of sciences

entries.title: Forschungszentrum Julich installs a D-Wave Advantage system at its JUNIQ facility, Europe's first quantum computer with more than 5,000 qubits.
IBM unveils Eagle, its first quantum processor to break the 100-qubit barrier at 127 qubits.
Alice & Bob installs a cryostat at its new Paris headquarters and readies Boson 1, its first cat-qubit chip, for testing.
Quantinuum's System Model H1-2 becomes the first quantum computer to pass a Quantum Volume of 2,048
IonQ launches Aria, a 21-qubit trapped-ion system, reaching a record 20 Algorithmic Qubits (#AQ20).
Alpine Quantum Technologies launches IBEX Q1, its first commercial rack-mounted trapped-ion quantum computer.
Alice & Bob announces its cat-qubit chip has achieved a 100-second bit-flip error-free record, alongside a 27 million euro Series A.
IonQ unveils Forte, its next-generation quantum computer, featuring a software-configurable acousto-optic-deflector laser system for individual qubit addressing.
Quantinuum completes a hardware upgrade to its H1 System Model, achieving 20 fully-connected trapped-ion qubits
Xanadu's Borealis photonic processor, using 216 squeezed-state qubits, becomes the first fully programmable photonic computer to demonstrate quantum computational advantage
Silicon Quantum Computing unveils the world's first integrated circuit manufactured with atomic precision, a 10-quantum-dot processor able to simulate the electron behavior of a small molecule.
Baidu releases its Qian Shi superconducting quantum computer and the Liang Xi hardware-software integration platform, and completes design of a 36-qubit chip.
IBM unveils Osprey, a 433-qubit processor more than tripling the qubit count of its 2021 Eagle chip.
Quandela deploys Arcturus, its first functional photonic quantum computer with 6 data qubits.
AQT's PINE Q1 trapped-ion system demonstrates a Quantum Volume of 128.
Alice & Bob performs its first inter-qubit operations on the Hydrogen chip series.

RIKEN, AIST, NICT, Osaka University, Fujitsu, and NTT announce Japan's first superconducting quantum computer, a 64-qubit chip on a 2cm die, and open it as a non-commercial cloud service to Japanese researchers.
entries.org: RIKEN; AIST; NICT; Osaka University; Fujitsu; NTT
entries.orgId: riken aist nict osaka university fujitsu ntt

entries.title: China's Zuchongzhi quantum computing platform expands from 66 to 176 qubits and opens to global users online.
entries.org: Chinese Academy of Sciences, University of Science and Technology of China, QuantumCTek
entries.orgId: chinese academy of sciences university of science and technology of china quantumctek

entries.title: Quantinuum launches System Model H2, a 32-qubit trapped-ion computer with a Quantum Volume of 65,536 and the largest GHZ-entangled state on record
IonQ launches Forte for commercial use, making #AQ29 performance available to customers worldwide.
Quantinuum's H1-1 system reaches a Quantum Volume of 524,288, a 1,000-fold jump over the next-best reported quantum volume
Infineon Technologies partners with eleQtron to co-develop three successive generations of trapped-ion quantum processor chips.

NEC and Tohoku University begin joint research using a newly built 8-qubit superconducting quantum annealing machine, Japan's first domestically manufactured annealer accessible remotely over the internet.
IQM and VTT complete Finland's second quantum computer, a 20-qubit superconducting system at VTT's Micronova facility in Espoo.
entries.org: IQM Quantum Computers, VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland
entries.orgId: iqm quantum computers vtt technical research centre of finland

entries.title: Fujitsu and RIKEN unveil a second 64-qubit superconducting quantum computer at their joint collaboration center and pair it with a 40-qubit simulator into a hybrid quantum computing platform offered to companies and research institutions.
IBM unveils Condor, a 1,121-qubit superconducting processor and the first quantum chip to surpass 1,000 qubits.
Rigetti launches Novera, its first commercially available quantum processing unit, a 9-qubit chip priced from $900,000 and built at Rigetti's Fab-1 facility.

Academia Sinica's self-developed 5-qubit superconducting quantum computer, with 99.9% gate fidelity, goes online for research collaborators, beating its original National Science and Technology Council target of a 3-qubit system by February 2024.
QuantWare's processor will power HectoQubit/2, a 100-qubit system being built at TU Delft and made accessible via QuTech's Quantum Inspire cloud.

Diraq and imec report a record 99.9% control accuracy for a silicon spin qubit fabricated on a 300mm wafer using standard CMOS materials, the highest fidelity yet for a silicon qubit made in a commercial foundry.

DRDO's Young Scientists Laboratory, TIFR Mumbai, and TCS complete end-to-end testing of an indigenous 6-qubit superconducting quantum processor built on a novel ring-resonator design, with a TCS-built cloud interface for running circuits.
entries.org: DRDO (DYSL-QT); Tata Institute of Fundamental Research; Tata Consultancy Services

entries.title: Google reports a random-circuit-sampling fidelity of 1.5x10^-3 at 67 qubits and 32 cycles, published in Nature as 'Phase transitions in random circuit sampling.'
IBM unveils Heron R2, a 156-qubit processor able to run circuits with up to 5,000 two-qubit gate operations.
IonQ agrees to acquire quantum-networking firm Qubitekk, expanding its entanglement-distribution and quantum-networking portfolio.

Turkiye unveils QuanT, its first domestically-built quantum computer, a 5-qubit system developed by TOBB University of Economics and Technology with support from the Presidency of Defense Industries and Aselsan.
entries.org: TOBB University of Economics and Technology (TOBB ETU), Presidency of Defense Industries (SSB), Aselsan
entries.orgId: tobb university of economics and technology tobb etu presidency of defense industries ssb aselsan

entries.title: Rigetti launches the 84-qubit Ankaa-3 system, achieving 99.5% median two-qubit gate fidelity via a new cryogenic design and ABAA junction-tuning technique.
Israel unveils its first domestically-built quantum computer, a 20-qubit superconducting system developed by Israel Aerospace Industries, Hebrew University and the Israel Innovation Authority.
entries.org: Israel Innovation Authority, Israel Aerospace Industries, Hebrew University/Yissum
entries.orgId: israel innovation authority israel aerospace industries hebrew university yissum

entries.title: A Warsaw University of Technology-led consortium, funded by Poland's National Center for Research and Development, advances a domestically-built military quantum computer prototype targeted for completion by the end of 2025.
entries.org: Warsaw University of Technology, Military University of Technology, National Center for Research and Development (NCBR)
entries.orgId: warsaw university of technology military university of technology national center for research and development ncbr

entries.title: Rigetti and Taiwan's Quanta Computer announce a strategic collaboration, each committing over $100 million over five years, to accelerate superconducting quantum computing commercialization.
Silicon Quantum Computing reports running Grover's search algorithm at 98.9% of its theoretical maximum accuracy on its atom-qubit processor with no error correction applied.
Silicon Quantum Computing announces record qubit read-out fidelities achieved at elevated operating temperatures, easing the cryogenic control burden for its atom-qubit platform.
IonQ completes acquisition of a controlling stake in Geneva-based ID Quantique, adding quantum-safe cryptography and sensing technology and over 900 combined patents.
D-Wave announces general availability of Advantage2, its sixth-generation annealing quantum computer with more than 4,400 qubits and doubled coherence
IBM announces it will build a new Quantum Data Center in Poughkeepsie, New York to house Starling, its planned large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computer targeted for 2029.
QBoson breaks ground on China's first dedicated photonic quantum computer factory in Shenzhen, planned to produce dozens of machines a year.
IonQ's 100-qubit Tempo system hits an algorithmic qubit score of #AQ 64, three months ahead of the company's own schedule.
IonQ completes its roughly $1.075 billion acquisition of UK trapped-ion chipmaker Oxford Ionics, with hardware operations required to remain in Britain under UK national security clearance.
Google's Willow chip runs the Quantum Echoes out-of-time-order-correlator algorithm 13,000x faster than the fastest classical supercomputer, a verifiable quantum advantage claim published in Nature.
IonQ completes its acquisition of Vector Atomic, a California-based quantum-sensing and precision-timing company.
IBM unveils Nighthawk, a 120-qubit processor with 218 tunable couplers designed to execute circuits with up to 5,000 two-qubit gates.
IBM unveils Loon, an experimental processor demonstrating all key components needed for fault-tolerant quantum computing, including new long-range 'c-couplers'.
Quantinuum launches Helios, a 98-qubit commercial system with 99.921% two-qubit gate fidelity delivering 48 error-corrected logical qubits
China Telecom's Tianyan cloud adds the 2,682-photon Tianyan-P2000 processor, making it the world's first cloud platform offering both photonic and superconducting quantum advantage services.
entries.org: China Telecom Quantum Group, Jiuzhang (Jinan) Quantum Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences
entries.orgId: china telecom quantum group jiuzhang jinan quantum technology chinese academy of sciences

entries.title: PsiQuantum breaks ground at its Moreton Bay Central site in Queensland, beginning construction of the facility intended to house the world's first utility-scale, fault-tolerant photonic quantum computer, with a large cryoplant from Linde Engineering due in late 2027.
IBM puts a 5-qubit quantum processor on the public cloud for the first time via the IBM Quantum Experience.
Volkswagen Group IT researchers use a D-Wave quantum computer to optimize traffic flow for 10,000 public taxis in Beijing, the automaker's first real-world quantum computing pilot.
IBM launches the IBM Q Network, giving JPMorgan Chase, Daimler, Samsung, and Oak Ridge National Laboratory cloud access to its 20-qubit quantum system.
entries.org: IBM, JPMorgan Chase, Daimler, Samsung, Oak Ridge National Laboratory
entries.orgId: ibm jpmorgan chase daimler samsung oak ridge national laboratory

entries.title: DENSO and Toyota Tsusho run what they call the world's first test applying a D-Wave quantum computer to real-time traffic data from roughly 130,000 commercial vehicles in Thailand.
ExxonMobil becomes the first energy company to join the IBM Q Network, partnering with IBM to research quantum algorithms for carbon-capture materials and maritime inventory routing.
Cambridge Quantum unveils IronBridge, the world's first commercially-ready certifiable quantum cryptographic device, at the RSA Conference
D-Wave sells the first of its next-generation 5,000-qubit quantum computer, later branded Advantage, to Los Alamos National Laboratory
entries.org: D-Wave Systems / Los Alamos National Laboratory
entries.orgId: d wave systems los alamos national laboratory

entries.title: Forschungszentrum Julich's JUNIQ facility signs a user contract for a D-Wave quantum annealer, becoming the first D-Wave Leap cloud-based quantum site in Europe.
Volkswagen runs the world's first live pilot of quantum-computed traffic-routing optimization for nine public buses, using a D-Wave system, at Web Summit in Lisbon
Woodside Energy becomes the first commercial organization in Australia to join the IBM Q Network, partnering with IBM to explore quantum computing for optimization problems across its operations.

American Banker reports Wells Fargo has signed a quantum computing research agreement with IBM and MIT, part of a program that has produced nearly a dozen quantum algorithms and ten peer-reviewed papers.
Amazon Web Services launches Amazon Braket, a managed quantum computing cloud service giving enterprise customers access to D-Wave, IonQ, and Rigetti hardware through a single platform.
Daimler AG and IBM use an IBM quantum computer to model the dipole moment of three lithium-containing molecules, a step toward next-generation lithium-sulfur EV batteries.
BBVA and Multiverse Computing pilot quantum and quantum-inspired algorithms on D-Wave, IBM Q System One, and Tensor Networks to optimize a 52-asset investment portfolio using 8 years of market data.
Volkswagen uses D-Wave's newly launched 5,000-qubit Advantage system to optimize paint-shop scheduling at its German production plants
Roche partners with Cambridge Quantum Computing to apply its EUMEN quantum chemistry platform to early-stage Alzheimer's disease drug discovery.
Boehringer Ingelheim becomes the first pharmaceutical company to partner with Google in quantum computing, launching a three-year collaboration on molecular dynamics simulation for drug R&D.
Microsoft brings Azure Quantum into public preview, giving enterprise customers cloud access to trapped-ion hardware from Honeywell and IonQ alongside Toshiba, Quantum Circuits, and 1QBit.
Barcelona's Qilimanjaro Quantum Tech partners with Abu Dhabi's Technology Innovation Institute to build the UAE's first quantum computer.
entries.org: Qilimanjaro Quantum Tech, Technology Innovation Institute
entries.orgId: qilimanjaro quantum tech technology innovation institute

entries.title: Goldman Sachs and QC Ware develop 'Shallow Monte Carlo' quantum algorithms for derivatives pricing designed to run on near-term quantum hardware, cutting the projected timeline to quantum advantage in half.
IBM and Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft unveil a 27-qubit IBM Quantum System One in Ehningen, Germany, the first IBM Quantum System One installed outside the United States.
Austria's Federal Ministry of Education, Science and Research launches the 'Quantum Austria' initiative, providing 107 million euros for quantum research and quantum technologies.
entries.org: Federal Ministry of Education, Science and Research (Austria)
entries.orgId: federal ministry of education science and research austria

entries.title: IBM and the University of Tokyo unveil Japan's first IBM Quantum System One, installed at the Kawasaki Business Incubation Center.
Oxford Quantum Circuits' 8-qubit Lucy processor becomes the first non-North-American quantum computer available on Amazon's AWS Braket cloud.
Cambridge Quantum launches Quantum Origin, a quantum-generated cryptographic key platform that becomes Quantinuum's first commercial cybersecurity product

BMW Group and AWS announce the winners of the BMW Group Quantum Computing Challenge, in which roughly 70 teams worldwide built quantum algorithms for sensor placement, material deformation, vehicle configuration, and quality-analysis problems.

Nomura Holdings, Nomura Securities, NICT, Toshiba, and NEC complete a joint verification test showing quantum cryptography can secure large-volume, low-latency stock-trading data transmission without degrading throughput versus a conventional system.

entries.title: Hyundai Motor partners with IonQ to build the most advanced battery-chemistry model yet run on a quantum computer, applying variational quantum eigensolver algorithms to lithium compound reactions.
Origin Quantum delivers the 24-qubit second-generation Wuyuan system to a consortium of investors, China's first commercially sold quantum computer.

Israel's Defense Ministry Directorate of Defense R&D (DDR&D) and the Israel Innovation Authority commit roughly NIS 200 million ($62 million) to establish the country's first national quantum computing center.

entries.title: Xanadu makes Borealis publicly available over Xanadu Cloud and Amazon Braket, the first cloud deployment of a quantum-advantage-capable photonic computer
D-Wave and Mastercard sign a multi-year strategic alliance to develop quantum-hybrid applications for loyalty, cross-border settlement and fraud management
Rigetti is awarded a $2.9 million DARPA contract, with the University of Technology Sydney, Aalto University and USC, to develop testable quantum application benchmarks.
IonQ signs a $13.4 million contract to supply trapped-ion quantum computing systems to the U.S. Air Force Research Laboratory.

entries.title: Universal Quantum wins a 67 million euro contract from Germany's DLR to build a fault-tolerant trapped-ion quantum computer of up to 100 qubits.
Eni and Pasqal partner to develop next-generation HPC-quantum computing solutions for Eni's upstream, refining, chemicals, and renewables operations, using Pasqal's neutral-atom systems.

JPMorgan Chase and Quantinuum run what they describe as the largest gate-based constrained-optimization demonstration to date, using Quantinuum's new 20-qubit H1-1 system on document-summarization-relevant workloads.
Cleveland Clinic and IBM unveil an on-site IBM Quantum System One, the first quantum computer in the world dedicated to healthcare research.
India's Union Cabinet approves the National Quantum Mission, allocating Rs 6,003.65 crore (about $730 million) through 2030-31 to build quantum computers and applications.

Rigetti delivers a 9-qubit Novera QPU to Horizon Quantum Computing for a new Singapore-based hardware testbed, its first QPU located in Asia.

Optimizing multiple-control Toffoli quantum circuit design with constraint programming
entries.countryEvidence: institution country codes [US, US, US] → US
entries.abstract: Abstract As quantum technology advances, the efficient design of quantum circuits has become an important area of research. This paper provides an introduction to the MCT quantum circuit design problem for reversible Boolean functions with the necessary background in quantum computing to comprehend the problem. While this is a well-studied problem, optimization models that minimize the true objective have only been explored recently. This paper introduces a new optimization model and symmetry-breaking constraints that improve solving time by up to two orders of magnitude compared to earlier work when a Constraint Programming solver is used. Experiments with up to seven qubits and using up to 15 quantum gates result in several new best-known circuits, obtained by any method, for well-known benchmarks. Several in-depth analyses are presented to validate the effectiveness of the symmetry-breaking constraints from multiple perspectives. Finally, an extensive comparison with other approaches shows that optimization models may require more time but can provide superior circuits with optimality guarantees.

entries.title: Quantum Federated Lasso With Anonymous Consensus Built Upon Quantum Blockchain
entries.countryEvidence: institution country codes [CN, CN, CN, CN, CN, CN] → CN
entries.abstract: ABSTRACT Objective With the rapid advancement of quantum computing technologies, Quantum Federated Learning (QFL) has emerged as a promising framework for privacy‐preserving quantum machine learning (QML). QFL provides a promising approach for privacy‐preserving quantum machine learning by enabling collaborative model training without sharing raw data. However, malicious or unreliable quantum clients may submit poisoned or low‐quality models, threatening the reliability of global aggregation. This study aims to develop a secure and robust QFL framework against such adversarial behaviors. Methods We propose a novel Quantum Federated Lasso (QFLasso) framework built on a quantum blockchain infrastructure. Local quantum models are trained with Lasso regularization to achieve sparse representations, and model updates are recorded on the blockchain. Quantum validators verify the quality and integrity of submitted models before aggregation. An anonymous quantum consensus mechanism is further introduced to ensure reliable agreement while preserving validator anonymity. Result The proposed framework integrates quantum Lasso optimization, federated learning, blockchain‐based verification, and anonymous quantum consensus to enhance the security, robustness, and privacy preservation of distributed quantum learning systems. The validator verification mechanism effectively filters unreliable model updates, while the anonymous consensus protocol improves resistance against dishonest or colluding validators and prevents targeted attacks on individual participants. Conclusion QFLasso provides a secure and scalable architecture for privacy‐preserving quantum intelligence, offering potential applications in secure quantum finance, distributed quantum sensing, and collaborative quantum medical diagnosis.

entries.title: Toward quantum computing gauge theories of nature
entries.org: Joint Center for Quantum Information and Computer Science
entries.countryEvidence: institution country codes [US, US, US] → US
entries.abstract: Abstract A hallmark of the computational campaign in nuclear and particle physics is the lattice-gauge-theory program. It continues to enable theoretical predictions for a range of phenomena in nature from the underlying Standard Model. The emergence of a new computational paradigm based on quantum computing, therefore, can introduce further advances in this program. In particular, it is believed that quantum computing will make possible first-principles studies of matter at extreme densities, and in and out of equilibrium, hence improving our theoretical description of the early universe, astrophysical environments, and high-energy particle collisions. Developing and advancing a quantum-computing-based lattice-gauge-theory program, therefore, have turned into a vibrant and fast-moving area of research in theoretical nuclear and particle physics. These lecture notes introduce the topic of quantum computing lattice gauge theories in a pedagogical manner, with an emphasis on theoretical and algorithmic aspects of the program, and on the most common approaches and practices, to keep the presentation focused and useful. The Hamiltonian formulation of lattice gauge theories is introduced within the Kogut–Susskind framework, the notion of Hilbert space and physical states is discussed, and some elementary numerical methods for performing Hamiltonian simulations are discussed. Quantum-simulation preliminaries and digital quantum-computing basics are presented, which set the stage for concrete examples of gauge-theory quantum-circuit design and resource analysis. A step-by-step analysis is provided for a simpler Abelian gauge theory, and an overview of our current understanding of the cost of quantum computing quantum chromodynamics is presented in the end. Examples and exercises supplement the material, and reinforce the concepts and methods introduced throughout.
entries.venue: The European Physical Journal Special Topics
entries.orgId: joint center for quantum information and computer science

entries.title: Parallel Multi‐Target Detection for Quantum LiDAR
entries.org: Pohang University of Science and Technology
entries.countryEvidence: institution country codes [KR, KR, KR, KR, KR, KR, KR] → KR
entries.abstract: ABSTRACT Quantum LiDAR can offer strong noise resilience in low‐light environments, but most prior demonstrations have relied on raster‐scanned telescope pointing, which interrogates target directions sequentially. Here, we present a wavelength‐randomness–driven beam‐steering scheme that enables parallel multi‐target detection. Using photon pairs generated via spontaneous four‐wave mixing, the probe photon is diffracted by a grating into a wavelength‐dependent angle, which varies from event to event due to intrinsic wavelength randomness. The arrival time of the heralding photon—delayed by propagation through a dispersive medium—encodes the probe wavelength and therefore identifies the corresponding diffraction direction. This time‐to‐angle mapping enables simultaneous interrogation of multiple targets, while the quantum‐correlated measurement yields up to a 1000‐fold signal‐to‐background noise improvement over classical LiDAR under comparable conditions. The corresponding signal‐to‐noise ratio (SNR) enhancement increases with background noise, consistent with theoretical predictions, although it does not exceed unity in the present experiment owing to the limited effective heralding efficiency. Our results establish a laboratory proof‐of‐principle architecture for parallel quantum‐enhanced low‐light sensing based on event‐wise angular encoding and coincidence‐based readout, with potential relevance to quantum metrology and correlated‐photon sensing applications.
entries.orgId: pohang university of science and technology

entries.title: Direct characterization of quantum dynamics via generalized weak values
entries.org: Collaborative Innovation Center of Advanced Microstructures
entries.countryEvidence: institution country codes [CN, CN, CN, CN, GB, CN, CN] → CN
entries.abstract: Weak values, emerging from weak measurements in pre- and postselection, exhibit complex-valued properties, enabling the direct characterization of quantum systems. However, conventional weak values do not account for system evolution, restricting their capability to capture quantum dynamical information. To overcome this limitation, we introduce the process weak value (PWV), which incorporates quantum evolution between observables during pre- and postselection. By establishing a connection between PWVs and the matrix elements of unitary operators, we develop a theoretical framework for the direct characterization of quantum processes. Our experimental validation encompasses single-photon and two-photon unitary processes, as well as non-Hermitian parity-time symmetric quantum processes. Compared to standard quantum process tomography, our method reduces the required bases for state preparation and measurements while circumventing complex reconstruction algorithms, enhancing efficiency and scalability. Our PWV formalism opens avenues for broader foundational investigations of weak measurements and enables efficient applications in characterizing sophisticated quantum processes.
entries.orgId: collaborative innovation center of advanced microstructures

entries.title: A longitudinal analysis of the CEC single-objective competitions (2010–2024) and implications for variational quantum optimization
End-to-end quantum algorithm for nonlinear fluid dynamics with bounded quantum advantage
Quantum machine learning for predicting properties of van der Waals bilayers
entries.abstract: Layering two-dimensional (2D) materials into van der Waals bilayers provides an effective method to achieve innovative quantum states and tunable electronic properties. Exploring the extensive configurational space resulting from various layer combinations, twist angles, and stacking patterns presents a significant computing challenge. As the volume of accessible datasets expands, training machine learning models on conventional hardware may ultimately become excessively expensive, prompting the advancement of quantum machine learning (QML) methodologies for materials discovery. This study presents a quantum-enhanced machine learning framework for predicting the bandgaps of van der Waals bilayers utilizing quantum simulators. We start with conventional feature selection to ascertain the most important descriptors affecting electronic properties. The properties are subsequently encoded into quantum states utilizing parameterized quantum circuits. We evaluated three quantum feature maps, namely ZFeatureMap, ZZFeatureMap, and PauliFeatureMap, and observed task-dependent performance. For the classification task, ZFeatureMap with two repetitions gave the strongest quantum-classifier performance, whereas for Quantum Support Vector Regressor (QSVR) bandgap regression, PauliFeatureMap with one repetition achieved the lowest average root-mean-square error (RMSE). These results indicate that feature-map choice should be optimized separately for classification and regression rather than being interpreted as the universal superiority of a single feature map. Using these feature-map comparisons, we employ a QSVR and a variational quantum regressor to predict the bandgaps of 1850 semiconducting vdW bilayers, illustrating that QML may attain significant predictive accuracy despite limited training data. Overall, the results demonstrate the feasibility of a simulator-based QML workflow for materials-property prediction. The quantum classifiers are competitive with the classical support vector classifier (SVC) baseline for zero-gap/non-zero-gap classification, whereas the classical Support Vector Regression (SVR) achieves the lowest RMSE in the regression task. Therefore, the present study should be interpreted as an exploratory benchmark of hybrid classical-quantum models for van der Waals (vdW) bilayers rather than as evidence of a definitive quantum performance advantage.

entries.title: Power-aware Lorentz-equivariant quantum graph neural network for accurate power estimation in CMOS-VLSI circuits
